title: Primitive Types 1
description: Rust has several primitive types. The boolean type `bool` can be either `true` or `false`.
instructions: Fix the code by creating the missing boolean variable.
hints: You need to declare a boolean variable called `is_evening`.
Boolean values in Rust are `true` and `false` (lowercase).
explanation: The `bool` type in Rust represents a boolean value, which can be either `true` or `false`. Booleans are used in conditions for `if` expressions, `while` loops, and `assert!` macros. They occupy one byte of memory.
